binary_metadata.file_type: a sh script, ASCII text executable

apt_metadata.description:
Windows Credentials Editor
Windows Credentials Editor (WCE) v1.3beta allows you to:
.
NTLM authentication:
.
* List logon sessions and add, change, list and delete associated credentials
(e.g.: LM/NT hashes)
* Perform pass-the-hash on Windows natively
* Obtain NT/LM hashes from memory (from interactive logons, services, remote
desktop connections, etc.) which can be used to authenticate to other systems.
WCE can perform this task without injecting code, just by reading and
decrypting information stored in Windows internal memory structures. It also
has the capability to automatically switch to code injection when the
aforementioned method cannot be performed.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use wce because ### wce.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair wce with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `wce --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### wce.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install wce
